title: Air France and Airbus convicted of manslaughter in 2009 Rio-Paris crash over Atlantic
content: On 1 June  2009, Air France Flight AF447, travelling from Rio de Janeiro to Paris, was cruising over the Atlantic when the pilots lost control of the aircraft, causing it to plunge into the ocean.
snippet: On 1 June 2009, Air France Flight AF447, travelling from Rio de Janeiro to Paris, was cruising over the Atlantic when the pilots lost control of the aircraft, causing it to plunge into the ocean.

title: Mother arrested after two French boys found abandoned in Portugal, police say
content: The boys were reported missing from their hometown of Colmar, in eastern France, by their father around 10 days ago.
snippet: The boys were reported missing from their hometown of Colmar, in eastern France, by their father around 10 days ago.

title: European carmakers face EU pressure to diversify chip suppliers
content: EU set to force carmakers to source chips from multiple suppliers after the Nexperia crisis exposed dangerous dependence on a single Chinese-linked provider.
snippet: EU set to force carmakers to source chips from multiple suppliers after the Nexperia crisis exposed dangerous dependence on a single Chinese-linked provider.

title: Merz's plan of 'associate EU membership' for Ukraine gets mixed reviews
content: Diplomats in Brussels are casting doubt over German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's bold plan to grant Ukraine "associate membership". The European Commission says any innovative solution should respect the "merit-based" nature of the multi-chapter accession process.

title: Spain to launch biggest forest fire campaign after record losses last year, Sánchez says
content: Spain is one of the southern European countries on the front line of climate change as higher average temperatures stoke heatwaves, droughts and forest fires.
snippet: Spain is one of the southern European countries on the front line of climate change as higher average temperatures stoke heatwaves, droughts and forest fires.

title: More money for the military - to do what? MEPs clash over Europe's defense spending on The Ring
content: Is higher defense spending in Europe a strategic investment or a random act to please Donald Trump? That's this week's topic of The Ring featuring MEPs Marc Botenga and Lukas Mandl as opponents.
snippet: Is higher defense spending in Europe a strategic investment or a random act to please Donald Trump? That's this week's topic of The Ring featuring MEPs Marc Botenga and Lukas Mandl as opponents.

title: Former UK pirate radio station sorry after mistakenly announcing death of King Charles
content: Established in 1964 to challenge the BBC's broadcasting monopoly, Radio Caroline previously operated from ships off the English coast.
snippet: Established in 1964 to challenge the BBC's broadcasting monopoly, Radio Caroline previously operated from ships off the English coast.

title: Cancer, oncogenic viruses found in wastewater: 'Possible breakthrough for prevention'
content: A new study has detected all major human cancer-causing viruses in wastewater, opening up fresh possibilities for tracking public health trends.
snippet: A new study has detected all major human cancer-causing viruses in wastewater, opening up fresh possibilities for tracking public health trends.

title: EU vows to stand firm on Russia as UK scrambles to explain sanctions decision
content: Brussels is testing the waters with G7 allies to introduce a full ban on maritime services for Russian oil tankers. But recent decisions taken by the US and the UK suggest an uphill struggle to secure the green light.

title: How Trump divided US companies – and handed Europeans an opening
content: Trump's aggressive push for US corporate interests in Europe is dividing American businesses and accelerating the EU's drive to cut off external dependencies, giving European companies a rare shot at markets long dominated by US giants.

title: ‘Each day counts’: EU scrambles to seal defence industry deal
content: European parliamentarians, EU member states and Commission mediators are in the thick of talks aimed at bolstering the bloc’s defence industry by cutting down on bureaucracy. Despite the political will for the continent to rearm itself, significant divisions have emerged on the details of delivery.

title: Merkel warns Europe can no longer rely on old security certainties
content: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel was awarded the European Order of Merit on Tuesday. Merkel warned that Europe could no longer take peace and security for granted following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. She also said changes in US security policy showed old certainties were fading.

title: EU keeps carbon border tax unchanged despite fertiliser price crisis
content: Fertiliser producers argue that the EU's carbon pricing rules at the border protect the European industry from cheaper imports produced under weaker environmental rules. But farmers fear they are indirectly paying the bill through higher fertiliser costs.

title: EU Order of Merit celebrates the same old memories and few fresh faces
content: The European Order of Merit conferral ceremony came off as a celebration of the past with little hope for the future. Merkel was among those awarded.
snippet: The European Order of Merit conferral ceremony came off as a celebration of the past with little hope for the future. Merkel was among those awarded.

title: NATO fighter jet downs stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia
content: For the first time, a NATO fighter jet shot down what is believed to have been a stray Ukrainian drone over the Baltic country.  Ukraine apologised for the “unintended incident”, while Russia threatened retaliation.

title: EU's largest party threatens to sanction MEP for appearing with far right
content: The European People’s Party is considering sanctions against its MEP Branko Grims after he took part in an event alongside far-right lawmakers. “It is the most normal thing,” he told Euronews, pointing to growing alignment on migration issues among right-wing political forces.

title: 'No way out': Italian divers who died in the Maldives may have become lost in cave, rescuers say
content: The divers went missing while "attempting to explore caves at a depth of 50 metres," the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs previously said.
snippet: The divers went missing while "attempting to explore caves at a depth of 50 metres," the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs previously said.

title: EU countries urge sanctions on Israeli minister for video of Gaza flotilla activists
content: The video even drew a sharp rebuke from senior politicians within Israel’s government with both Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar slamming its publication.
snippet: The video even drew a sharp rebuke from senior politicians within Israel’s government with both Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar slamming its publication.

title: Turning point: Germans lose faith in US as NATO ally and turn to defence, study shows
content: A German defence survey suggests that trust in the US as a NATO ally has fallen sharply, while support for higher defence spending and the military has continued to rise.
snippet: A German defence survey suggests that trust in the US as a NATO ally has fallen sharply, while support for higher defence spending and the military has continued to rise.

title: Russia delivers nuclear warheads to Belarus as NATO tensions spike
content: Russia has delivered nuclear munitions ‌to field storage facilities in Belarus as part of major nuclear drills amid tensions with European NATO members sparked by drone incursions in the Baltic.
snippet: Russia has delivered nuclear munitions to field storage facilities in Belarus as part of major nuclear drills amid tensions with European NATO members sparked by drone incursions in the Baltic.

title: EU cuts 2026 growth forecast as Strait of Hormuz crisis pushes inflation up
content: The EU has downgraded its 2026 economic growth forecast after the Strait of Hormuz crisis sent energy prices sharply higher earlier this year. With no end to the conflict in sight, rising energy costs are fuelling tensions among member states calling for additional fiscal support measures.

title: EU countries and Parliament clash on controversial return rules for migrants
content: The European Parliament and EU member states have failed to agree on a law enabling home searches and setting up deportation centres outside the EU to speed up migrants’ returns.
snippet: The European Parliament and EU member states have failed to agree on a law enabling home searches and setting up deportation centres outside the EU to speed up migrants’ returns.

title: Latvia issues warning to residents after detecting unmanned aerial vehicle in its airspace

title: No, the US hasn't announced a total withdrawal of troops from Europe
content: Amid real reports of the US scaling back its military presence in European countries, there are also false claims that Washington is removing the entirety of its personnel from the continent.

title: Italy moves to Airbus A330 tankers in major NATO-aligned shift

title: Merz proposes 'associate EU membership' for Ukraine, with mutual assistance
content: The "associate membership" envisioned by Friedrich Merz would allow Ukraine to request assistance from other EU countries against a Russian attack. This, Merz says, would create a "substantial security guarantee".

title: Technological sovereignty is not about isolation, EU digital chief says
content: Henna Virkkunen says tech sovereignty means cutting risky dependencies and boosting European capacity in areas from semiconductor supply chains to cloud services.
snippet: Henna Virkkunen says tech sovereignty means cutting risky dependencies and boosting European capacity in areas from semiconductor supply chains to cloud services.

title: Paris nursery abuse probe expands as 16 staff held over extended custody
content: The suspects worked at the Saint-Dominique state nursery school in Paris’ 7th arrondissement and are being questioned over rape and other allegedly excessive or violent behaviour.
snippet: The suspects worked at the Saint-Dominique state nursery school in Paris’ 7th arrondissement and are being questioned over rape and other allegedly excessive or violent behaviour.

title: Baltics must adapt to drone incursions because more will come, Lithuanian defence minister warns
content: Exclusive: As the Baltics continue to be rattled by repeated drone incidents – with at least six actual or suspected incidents rocking the region this month – Lithuania’s Minister of Defence Robertas Kaunas told Euronews there is a “high possibility” more unmanned aerial incidents will materalise.

title: Marking 20 years of independence, Montenegro eyes EU as next milestone
content: Concerts and various celebrations are being held in the capital Podgorica this week and other towns in Montenegro, which is known for its stunning Adriatic Sea coastline and towering mountains.
snippet: Concerts and various celebrations are being held in the capital Podgorica this week and other towns in Montenegro, which is known for its stunning Adriatic Sea coastline and towering mountains.

title: Naples woken by strong earthquake as tremors hit city
content: A 4.4-magnitude tremor was recorded at dawn on Thursday in Naples. INGV reports that the quake, with its epicentre in the Gulf of Pozzuoli, was 3 kilometres deep.
snippet: A 4.4-magnitude tremor was recorded at dawn on Thursday in Naples. INGV reports that the quake, with its epicentre in the Gulf of Pozzuoli, was 3 kilometres deep.

title: The EU simplified its toughest AI law: what changed and why it matters

title: Europe Today: Drones, bunkers and political turmoil: what’s going on in Lithuania?
content: A drone incursion into Lithuania on Wednesday brought the capital to a standstill and forced the country’s leaders to shelter in a bunker. Ursula von der Leyen has said Europe will respond with “unity and strength”. We speak to Lithuania’s defence minister, Robertas Kaunas.

title: United States: sanctions lifted on UN rapporteur Francesca Albanese
content: The US Treasury Department has announced that sanctions against UN rapporteur Francesca Albanese have been lifted. Last week, a federal judge ordered their suspension after upholding an appeal lodged by Albanese’s relatives.

title: Germany targets major stake in Europe’s tank titan KNDS
content: The KNDS weapons group is a merger between France’s Nexter and Germany’s Krauss-Maffei Wegmann weapons firms. The German state is set to initially own 40% of the company with plans to reduce its stake to 30% within two to three years.

title: EU migrant repatriations on the rise, but which countries are carrying out the most explusions?

title: European governments slam Israel's treatment of flotilla activists as 'unacceptable'

title: Can Europe secure its own future in a world of great power politics?

title: Visegrád group eyes revival with post-Orbán Hungary
content: A revival of the Visegrád group is on the cards as Hungary's new PM Péter Magyar moves to reset relations with his neighbours. Whether the V4 can reclaim real clout in Brussels is another question — the bloc plans to align on issues case by case, pulling in other European countries as needed.

title: Ukraine and Hungary move to resolve deadlock over minority rights that has stalled Kyiv’s EU bid
